votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the logic of counting uppercase vowels at even indices, matching the Coq specification's recursive structure and character checks.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq functions, correctly counting uppercase vowels at even indices and matching the specification.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the helper functions and the recursive counting logic for uppercase vowels at even indices.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq code. is_upper_vowel checks the same 5 uppercase vowels. string_to_list converts a string to a list of characters. count_upper_vowels_even_indices_aux recursively processes the list, checking if the index is even (using idx % 2 == 0, which matches Nat.even) and if the character is an upper vowel, counting accordingly. count_upper_spec returns a boolean indicating whether result equals the computed count, which matches the Coq Prop semantically for concrete inputs.

Both implementations count uppercase vowels A,E,I,O,U at even (0-based) indices; string-to-list conversion and evenness checks align, yielding the same result on all corresponding inputs.
